$comment: SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 | Copyright 2026 OKPF Contributors — PLACEHOLDER: all numeric values are illustrative, not from a real training run
description: Representation metadata for a JEPA artifact pack example. Describes the representation space, architecture components, and training objective. No model weights are included or referenced.
reference: Assran et al., 2023 — Self-Supervised Learning from Images with a Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture

representation.notes: Representations are learned in latent space. No pixel-level reconstruction is performed.

context_encoder.notes: PLACEHOLDER parameter count. Processes the visible (unmasked) context patches.

target_encoder.notes: PLACEHOLDER parameter count and EMA decay. Not trained by gradient descent; updated from the context encoder.

predictor.description: Predicts target encoder patch representations from context encoder representations, conditioned on the positions of the masked target blocks.

training_objective.name: JEPA latent prediction
training_objective.loss: L2 in representation space (no stop-gradient on predictor output, EMA stop-gradient on target)
training_objective.notes: PLACEHOLDER mask scales. The key property of JEPA: prediction is done in abstract representation space, not pixel space.

okpf_notes: OKPF does not define neural architectures, inference engines, or weight formats. This file describes the representation space for metadata and discoverability purposes. Consumers requiring inference must load weights using the appropriate ML framework independently of OKPF.
